narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 c.502C>G (p.Pro168Ala) missense change is a rare variant that has been identified in breast‑cancer cohorts from the United States and Jordan and in a Vietnamese colorectal‑cancer cohort, but it has not been linked to a specific hereditary cancer syndrome [PMC8989924][PMC9792166].
Clinical reports consistently classify the alteration as a variant of uncertain significance. In a 2022 study of breast‑cancer patients undergoing genetic testing the variant was observed in a small number of cases and was not considered to influence surgical or therapeutic decisions [PMC8989924]. A 2018 investigation of high‑risk Jordanian breast‑cancer patients listed P168A as a VUS, and a 2021 survey of young Jordanian breast‑cancer patients similarly recorded it among several VUS without providing functional data [PMC5802063][PMC8295261]. The Vietnamese colorectal‑cancer cohort also reported the change as an uncertain‑significance finding in circulating‑tumor‑DNA profiling [PMC9792166].
Functional data are lacking. A comprehensive annotation of all possible BRCA2 missense variants includes P168A but does not provide experimental evidence regarding its effect on protein function or cellular phenotype [PMC6287763].
Computational predictions favor a benign interpretation. One in‑silico analysis using multiple predictive scores assigned the P168A variant a benign classification, with a high MetaSVM score and other metrics supporting low pathogenic potential [PMC9213547]. No population‑frequency data beyond the small case counts are reported, but the variant appears only sporadically in the cited cohorts.
Taken together, the variant is repeatedly reported as a VUS in clinical settings, lacks experimental functional validation, and is predicted to be benign by computational models. In the absence of definitive segregation, functional, or population‑frequency evidence, the current consensus is that BRCA2 p.Pro168Ala remains a variant of uncertain significance, with a leaning toward a likely benign effect.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports the BRCA2 p.Pro168Ala variant as a missense VUS observed in a small number of breast‑cancer patients, with no impact on surgical or therapeutic decisions.
article_mentions.title: Rates of Variants of Uncertain Significance Among Patients With Breast Cancer Undergoing Genetic Testing: Regional Perspectives

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: In a cohort of 616 young Jordanian breast‑cancer patients, 12.2% carried pathogenic/likely‑pathogenic BRCA1/2 mutations and the study lists several VUS, including BRCA2 p.Pro168Ala, without further functional evidence.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence and predictors of germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations among young patients with breast cancer in Jordan

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article’s computational model predicts the BRCA2 P168A missense variant to be benign.
article_mentions.title: Understanding and predicting the functional consequences of missense mutations in BRCA1 and BRCA2

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article presents a comprehensive functional and computational annotation of all possible BRCA2 missense variants, including P168A, and introduces calibrated meta‑predictors that improve pathogenicity prediction accuracy.
article_mentions.title: Comprehensive annotation of BRCA1 and BRCA2 missense variants by functionally validated sequence-based computational prediction models

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article describes a computational framework that screens SNPs in BRCA2 and other TNBC‑associated genes, predicting a substantial proportion of them to be pathogenic, and highlights potential clinical relevance through drug‑annotation tables.
article_mentions.title: Analysis of single-nucleotide polymorphisms in genes associated with triple-negative breast cancer

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The Vietnamese colorectal cancer cohort was genomically profiled, revealing the BRCA2 p.P168A variant as a variant of uncertain significance and demonstrating the feasibility of personalized ctDNA monitoring for recurrence detection.
article_mentions.title: Tumor genomic profiling and personalized tracking of circulating tumor DNA in Vietnamese colorectal cancer patients
